data.title: Safe Addresses Security Concerns Following Bybit Wallet Incident
data.description: According to Foresight News, the multi-signature wallet protocol Safe has confirmed that all transaction information for Bybit wallets is accurately displayed on the front end, including the execution of malicious transactions with all signatures. Safe reported no leaks in its codebase or malicious dependencies, and no other Safe addresses have been affected. Following the incident, Safe has temporarily suspended its Wallet functionality to thoroughly inspect service configurations and replace infrastructure credentials. The company is also collaborating with external security researchers to audit its codebase.

data.title: Bybit Loses Significant Assets in Cyberattack
data.description: According to Foresight News, Lookonchain reported on the X platform that Bybit had $16.2 billion in reserve assets before a recent cyberattack. Data from CoinMarketCap indicates that approximately $1.4 billion was stolen, accounting for about 8.64% of the total reserves.

data.title: CZ: Binance does not yet officially support Bybit, and related transfers are spontaneous actions of users
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: In response to the community's concern about "Binance is providing ETH loans to Bybit", Binance founder CZ responded on the X platform that Binance has not yet started to support Bybit. These are spontaneous transactions by users. It may be that some whales lent money to Bybit. Binance cannot take credit for it. Previously, CZ suggested that Bybit suspend withdrawals, saying that "$1.5 billion in stolen assets is enough to be terrifying. It is better to be safe than sorry now."

data.title: MEXC hot wallet transferred 12,652 stETH to Bybit, and Bybit has now received 64,000 ETH asset support
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst Yu Jin, MEXC hot wallet transferred 12,652 stETH (about 33.75 million US dollars) to Bybit cold wallet in the past hour. Bybit should have received 64,452 ETH (about 170 million US dollars) of loan support from Bitget, an institution that withdrew funds from Binance, and MEXC.

data.title: Safe: No security vulnerabilities were found after a comprehensive investigation, and other Safe addresses are not affected
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News In response to the incident of "ByBit multi-signature wallet causing $1.5 billion in assets to be stolen due to malicious manipulation", the multi-signature wallet platform Safe issued a statement on the X platform: "· No code base leakage was found: the Safe code base was thoroughly checked and no evidence of leakage or modification was found. 
· No malicious dependencies were found: there is no indication that malicious dependencies in the Safe code base will affect transaction flow (i.e. supply chain attack) 
· No unauthorized access to the infrastructure was detected in the logs 
· No other Safe addresses were affected Safe said that it has temporarily suspended the Safe{Wallet} function to ensure that users have absolute confidence in the security of the Safe platform. 
Although the investigation showed no evidence that the Safe{Wallet} front end itself was compromised, we are conducting a more thorough review."

data.title: Bybit Hacker's cmETH Withdrawal Rejected, Funds Remain Unmoved
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on February 22, on-chain data analyst Yu Jin reported that an hour ago, a withdrawal request for 15,000 cmETH by the Bybit hacker was rejected by the cmETH withdrawal contract. Following this, the hacker authorized a transaction for cmETH on DODO but did not proceed further, possibly due to the shallow liquidity pool of cmETH.

The 15,000 cmETH appears to have been intercepted. In addition to this amount, the total stolen ETH from Bybit amounts to 499,000 ETH, valued at approximately $1.37 billion, which has been distributed across 51 different addresses by the hacker.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 650 USDT with a Narrowed 0.38% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Feb 22, 2025, 01:24 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 650 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 650.049988 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 0.38% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Bybit’s application to unstake 15,000 cmETH stolen was rejected, but may be successfully intercepted
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of chain data analyst Yu Jin, 1 hour ago, the Bybit hacker's 15,000 cmETH unstaking application was returned by the cmETH withdrawal contract. After that, the hacker authorized the transaction of cmETH on DODO, but there was no further transaction, which may be caused by the very shallow liquidity pool of cmETH. These 15,000 cmETH should be intercepted.
In addition to these 15,000 cmETH, the number of stolen ETH from Bybit is 499,000 (about 1.37 billion US dollars), which was dispersed and stored in 51 addresses by hackers.

data.title: Bybit CEO Addresses Withdrawal Surge Following Cyberattack
data.description:
According to Odaily, Bybit co-founder and CEO Ben Zhou recently shared on Platform X that the company has experienced an unprecedented number of withdrawal requests following a cyberattack that occurred 10 hours ago. Zhou reported that Bybit received over 350,000 withdrawal requests, with approximately 2,100 still pending. He assured that 99.994% of the requests have been successfully processed. Zhou encouraged users to comment if their withdrawals have been completed.

Despite what Zhou described as potentially the most severe cyberattack in the history of media, including banking, cryptocurrency, and finance sectors, all Bybit functions and products remain operational. The entire team has been working tirelessly through the night to address customer inquiries and concerns, demonstrating full commitment to resolving the situation. Zhou reassured users of the company's support and solidarity during this challenging time.

data.title: Bitget CEO: I believe Bybit customer funds are 100% safe, no need to panic or run
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bitget CEO Gracy Chen posted a message on the X platform to support Bybit, saying: "Bybit is a respectable competitor and partner. Although the loss this time is huge, it is only their annual profit. I believe that customer funds are 100% safe. There is no need to panic or run."
